item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this, but I think it would be more effective with more information. As people said before, mentioning Sinclair was under surveillance would make sense, but I think mentioning the disappearance thing earlier would make it more intense (if all the pens in the facility disappeared after the incident, what's going to happen to the family?)
That's just my super subjective opinion of what would make it a little better, it's super well done and I gave it a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favourite items on this site, everything about this is phenomanol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god, this really caught me off guard. The manifestation log completely convinced me that this was one of the lighthearted SCPs, and that only made what came next so much more horrifying. To the point, impactful, and made me choke on my dinner.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god that ending. Help me I cant choose between my three barf bags! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: don't we all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For once I have nothing to say other than good job.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a fan at first, as I found the whole thing to be a bit not very good, but that all changed when the fire nation attacked I got to the ending. +1

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded: It's because as a member of staff one might assume that he is speaking on behalf of the rest of staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He was being choked because he believed he needed air, which was located inside of the manifestation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn this is amazing

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded: why do you even need that disclaimer? anyone who genuinely thinks one person is acting for an entire site is insane and shouldn't even be on this site in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the concept and execution of the idea very much, but the twist and the incident log at the end did not work for me. Apart from the fact that what Sinclair wanted was actually inside the manifestation unlike previous incidents described, the vibe it ended in was vastly different from what's before it. The stakes/consequences just went up too much for my liking. The twist was in a direction that I do not like. It is a natural extension of the effects of the phenomenon, but it did lost me by the end.

+1 anyway

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I hate to be so blunt as to ask to have it explained (re: why the disappearances at the end, but more why he actually was inside the kid, not to mention wanted to find himself at the moment). I may be way overthinking this, feels like the transcendence is/inside thing…

But could you at least say if there is a specific answer that wasn’t intentionally left to interpretation? It’s been gnawing at me for awhile.

I’ll upvote for that alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This went from silly, to hilarious, to downright disturbing as it went on. I honestly am a bit…terrified now.
Excuse me for a moment, I have to break open my cellphone to find my car keys.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote a comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it, though the use of phenomena as singular early on distracted me for a bit …

Also, I don't follow what was going on at the dinner table. Usually the manifestation isn't a copy of one of the things being chosen from, but this time it was? Why did he appear in there, and how was he being choked? It's very creepy, but it doesn't hold together in a satisfying way.

Oh, and unless the site director lives in a pretty remote area, the disappearance of all humans in a 1km radius would be a Broken Masquerade scenario for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt like the pacing here undermines the rest. We’re too quickly up from a broken pen to a broken person. Which is a shame because I like this concept but the rush to get to the end makes it weaker than it should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that was suitably alarming

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, that ending threw me for a loop. Awesome concept, with my only critique already being stated by other members (being the camera's in the Site Director's home).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
making a choice between three similar objects or entities.

Kind of confused on that part.

Damn, that ending twist is good. A cliffhanger but a good one. At first I thought he was just gonna keep going and find nothing, but I was wrong. I was so wrong.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hold on i think i left my upvote inside the computer i need to ju

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aghhhhh I had a feeling that’s where you were going but still, that was very effectively creepy.

+1

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded:
I reflexively went "Whaaaaat?" towards the end.

+[Critique] Click to Access
-[Click to Hide Text]

Before I get too far, you refer to "Dr. Carè" as "Dr. West" in the incident description. Probably unintentional.

This is a very creative premise and I feel as if it does compulsion in a very sneaky way. Each thing trapped in the fourth object is always something that the person needs at the time, so it feels almost natural for the person to destroy whatever is in the way of them getting to it. As soon as we get to Sinclair in the Manifestation log, I started feeling the dread.

I feel like it would have been useful at this point to mention that Sinclair's residence is under surveillance, since it seems kind of awkward for there to be a log set in someone's private home. I can make that stretch make sense if I believe that Site Director's are monitored due to the importance of their position, so it didn't totally take me out of it. And the way the incident escalates kept me locked into place. The visceral detail and the raw desperation coming off of Sinclair is borderline madness, and to have it cap off the way that it did threw me across the room. I'll admit that I don't know the significance of it, but the reaction it got out of me alone was enough to push me into upvote territory.

TL;DR I +1'd because the incident log felt like a short film horror movie. I paid for the whole seat, but I really only used the edge.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to RockTeethMothEyes or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My immediate reaction was:

"What the fuck? That was…what the fuck? WHAT?"

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The Season begins anew. There will be no escape for any of you.

Thanks to Cerastes, ParadoxPotentia and Radguel for their feedback!
